body:
## Summary
- ARO HCP does not need router LoadBalancer services since Swift handles connectivity
- PR #7845 introduced a regression by creating LB services for ARO HCP with Swift enabled
- Adds `IsAroHCP()` check to `reconcileHCPRouterServices` so router LB services are always skipped for ARO HCP
- Adds test case for ARO with Swift ensuring no services are created

This was introduced via https://github.com/openshift/hypershift/pull/7845

## Test plan
- [x] Unit tests pass (`go test ./control-plane-operator/controllers/hostedcontrolplane/infra/...`)
- [x] Testdata regenerated with `UPDATE=true`
- [ ] Verify no router LB services are created for ARO HCP clusters

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Corrected router service handling for ARO HostedControlPlanes to ensure appropriate infrastructure management and service cleanup.

* **Tests**
  * Added test coverage validating proper service behavior when ARO HostedControlPlanes are configured.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: OCPBUGS-81162: fix(cpo): skip router LB services for ARO HCP
